wiki.summary: "Lucid Dreams" is a song by rapper & singer Juice WRLD, it originally released on June 15, 2017 as apart of his 999 EP before being re-released on May 4, 2018 as the second single to his debut album, Goodbye & Good Riddance. The song was produced by Nick Mira & SIDEPCE, prominently sampling Sting's "Shape Of My Heart" (1993). Lucid Dreams ranked at #12 on Billboard's Year-End list of 2018 & is currently certified 11× Platinum by the RIAA, making it eligible for Diamond status. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Lucid Dreams" is a song by rapper & singer Juice WRLD, it originally released on June 15, 2017 as apart of his 999 EP before being re-released on May 4, 2018 as the second single to his debut album, Goodbye & Good Riddance. The song was produced by Nick Mira & SIDEPCE, prominently sampling Sting's "Shape Of My Heart" (1993). Lucid Dreams ranked at #12 on Billboard's Year-End list of 2018 & is currently certified 11× Platinum by the RIAA, making it eligible for Diamond status. On the track, Juice WRLD explores the mental anguish someone feels after experiencing a break-up. Lucid dreaming refers to a state of consciousness where a person is aware they are dreaming. With enough experience, a dreamer can slowly begin to control the contents of their dreams. Juice uses the idea of controlling a lucid dream as an analogy for his mental state; to avoid the pain of seeing his ex-lover in his dreams, Juice seeks to replace them—for good. In a June 2018 interview with Lyrical Lemonade, Juice stated the song was made to be a "therapy session" during a period of relationship problems he was encountering. In the lyrics, he describes the pain of a girl breaking up with him, and in the verse he relates back to the title: saying he has lucid dreams thinking of her. Lucid Dreams was initially released on SoundCloud on June 15, 2017 under the title "Lucid Dreams (Forget Me)", alongside the EP JuiceWrld 9 9 9. It eventually accumulated 2.5 million plays, leading to an official release as a single on May 4, 2018. In November 2018, producer Nick Mira revealed that Sting owned 85% of the rights to 'Lucid Dreams', which features an interpolation of his 1993 hit "Shape of My Heart". Fellow rapper Lil Uzi Vert appears on the song's remix. Lucid Dreams debuted at #74 on the Billboard Hot 100 on the week ending May 26, 2018, with 10 million US streams and 4,000 digital downloads. The song jumped to #35 the following week and then to #15. When it ascended from #15 to #9, Lucid Dreams became Juice's first top 10 single on the week of June 16, 2018 with over 35.9 million US streams and 9,000 downloads. It slowly climbed to #3, maintaining a spot in the top 10 for months until it peaked at #2 for 2 non-consecutive weeks in October 2018, prevented from reaching the top spot by Maroon 5's Girls Like You featuring Cardi B. Following Juice Wrld's death in December 2019, Lucid Dreams re-entered the Hot 100 at #8, becoming it's 26th nonconsecutive week in the top-10. During the 2019 Grammy telecast, host Alicia Keys performed 30 seconds of Lucid Dreams, singing & playing piano, during a segment on songs about heartache. The song's accompanying music video was directed by Cole Bennett, released & uploaded on Lyrical Lemonade's YouTube channel on May 11, 2018. It features Juice trapped up to his head in a floor, with his body tied down, and has been described by as "psychedelic and abstract". The music video reached a billion views on August 7, 2024, and as of 2026, the music video has over 1.1 billion views on YouTube. The song has over 3 billion streams on Spotify, being one of the most-streamed songs on the platform. Read more on Last.fm.
